title: Confidence intervals + bootstrapping + DSA Two Pointers
focus: CIs, bootstrap, frequentist vs Bayesian; start the Two Pointers pattern.

items.label: Valid Palindrome
interviewQuestions: How do you handle non-alphanumerics and case — what's your filter logic?
What if the string is huge and streamed — can you process it without loading it all?

items.label: Two Sum II Input Array IS Sorted
interviewQuestions: What invariant do the two pointers preserve, and why does that prove correctness?
Why is this O(n) and not O(n²) — what guarantees each element is visited a bounded number of times?
Walk through edge cases: empty input, all duplicates, sorted vs unsorted.

items.label: 3sum
interviewQuestions: How do you avoid duplicate triples without a set — what's the skip-duplicates pattern?
Generalize to k-sum: what's the recursion, and what's the complexity?

items.label: Confidence intervals: meaning, construction, common misreadings
interviewQuestions: What does a 95% confidence interval actually mean?
Why is a 95% CI NOT 'a 95% probability the true value is in this range' under frequentist statistics?
How would you build a CI for a click-through rate with N=1,000?

items.label: Bootstrap & permutation tests
interviewQuestions: When is bootstrapping more useful than a parametric CI?
Walk through bootstrapping a CI for the median.
How does a permutation test compare to a t-test when assumptions are violated?

items.label: Frequentist vs Bayesian intervals
interviewQuestions: Compare frequentist confidence intervals vs Bayesian credible intervals.
When does a Bayesian credible interval communicate uncertainty more naturally?
